title: NO SPOILERS 30 coming in January
wikitext:
[]: [NO SPOILERS] 3.0 coming in January?             Quote Post by chee » Tue Nov 20, 2012 8:34 pm http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0860907/releaseinfo SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Tue Nov 20, 2012 8:52 pm I don't know what IMDB's policy on dates is, any chance that's just them making guesstimates?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Nov 20, 2012 8:56 pm If that's true I'm about as jealous as I can get. I won't have a chance to see it on big screen untill next HIFF from nearly year from now (and that's presuming they get 3.0 for showing)

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Tue Nov 20, 2012 9:06 pm CJD wrote: I don't know what IMDB's policy on dates is, any chance that's just them making guesstimates?

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Tue Nov 20, 2012 9:13 pm Nope, no dates confirmed by Madman yet. I doubt we're gunna get any solid information on this for a little while. i.e. IMDB is full of shit.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Tue Nov 20, 2012 9:18 pm The IMBD page doesn't have any official announcements tied to it. I do not believe there will be any announcements for screenings outside of Japan until the film has finished screening in Japan. As for the Art Gallery that's coming to Australia from April next year, there have been no announcements about a screening or theatre run tied to that event. It is possible that Madman are in discussions with Khara for it, but they haven't said anything beyond their statement at the SMASH! 2012 Q&A panel which I don't want to look up right now.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Tue Nov 20, 2012 9:19 pm American distributor for theatrical run is Eleven Arts. They haven't said anything about 3.0 to my knowledge.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Nov 20, 2012 10:17 pm The only way there will be a theatrical engagement is if one of two things happens: 1) It gets licensed by a US distributor (Funimation), which probably won't happen until after the video release, and after they dub it. They won't release it subbed. 2) It gets included in a specific film festival. IMDB doesn't really track these engagements. I really don't this IMDB stuff is accurate. It's way too early for this to come together.

[]:             Quote Post by Redtophat » Tue Nov 20, 2012 10:20 pm How would they show it without a distributor? My hopes were up thinking Funimation picked it up early due to it's record-breaking box office sales in Japan but now... :(

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Tue Nov 20, 2012 10:35 pm *Eleven Arts is the theatrical distributor.* Has been that way with 1.0 and 2.0. The theatrical run was always done before Funimation released the video version, and it was a very limited release. Most likely will be that way for 3.0 as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Redtophat » Tue Nov 20, 2012 11:15 pm JoeD80 wrote: *Eleven Arts is the theatrical distributor.* Has been that way with 1.0 and 2.0. The theatrical run was always done before Funimation released the video version, and it was a very limited release. Most likely will be that way for 3.0 as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Unit88 » Tue Nov 20, 2012 11:51 pm I saw rebuild 1.0 in at the Plaza Theater in Atlanta and I think it was subbed actually... No I take that back... It WAS dubbed... I remember thinking how all the voice actors sounded close to the originals but how Kensuke was almost annoying. So yeah... It was dubbed. Gosh that was a while back...

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Wed Nov 21, 2012 12:59 am The Eva Monkey wrote: 1) It gets licensed by a US distributor (Funimation), which probably won't happen until after the video release, and after they dub it. They won't release it subbed.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Wed Nov 21, 2012 1:29 am Electric Sachiel wrote: Funimation released 2.0 in theaters subbed before they released it on dvd and blu ray.

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Wed Nov 21, 2012 9:39 am Imbd is like wikipidea, so its just some guy trying to be an asshole

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Nov 21, 2012 12:20 pm IMDB like Wikipedia is User Run. Members of the website add information regarding films. Usually a production assistant on a movie will be given the duty of entering studio approved names and credits on a films IMDB page but it's very rare. When Evangelion 3.0 gets a US theartrical run we'll hear about it from Funimation first. PS. The greatest example of IMDB 'What-the-fuckery' misuse actually is Evangelion related. A few years ago the still-hasn't-happened 'Live Action Neon Genesis Evangelion' IMDB page suddenly had a writer & director attached. A certain Spencer David. Who is Spencer David you ask? ..... He was a 17 year old rich kid from the suburbs who posted in the forums and had added his name as director becuase he ASSURED everyone that he had talked to Steven Spielberg - the 'supposed owner' of Evangelion - and in a years time would have a Trilogy in production. It was pretty hilarious. He even had a website: http://spencerdavidfilms.weebly.com/pre-production.html There is even a screenplay: http://www.scribd.com/doc/16390459/Spencer-Davids-Neon-Genesis-Evangelion Moral of the story: If IMDB is the only place a piece of movie news exists.... it's not real.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Wed Nov 21, 2012 12:25 pm ^This Spencer David kid sounds like a Tommy Wiseau in the making. If that's true, he'd better stay the hell away from Evangelion if he doesn't want a fan-valanche to bear down on his home.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Wed Nov 21, 2012 12:32 pm ^ when this gets a international release Madman or Funi will be our source, that's the only reason that I check they're blog. Also "you're tearing me apart Asuka!"

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Wed Nov 21, 2012 12:38 pm bobbyfischer's ghost wrote: Also "you're tearing me apart Asuka!"

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Nov 21, 2012 5:16 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: The only way there will be a theatrical engagement is if one of two things happens: 1) It gets licensed by a US distributor (Funimation), which probably won't happen until after the video release, and after they dub it. They won't release it subbed.
summary:
[]: [NO SPOILERS] 3.0 coming in January?             Quote Post by chee » Tue Nov 20, 2012 8:34 pm http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0860907/releaseinfo SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Tue Nov 20, 2012 8:52 pm I don't know what IMDB's policy on dates is, any chance that's just them making guesstimates?

[]:          &nbsp
